claim: YOLO demand evidence schema, protocols, readiness checks, and tests all distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes with explicit validation and blocker generation.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION defines evidence record structure with required scope field (project|external|user|unknown) and source field (project_code|project_test|project_docs|project_config|project_log|project_artifact|external_web|external_docs|user|unknown)
evidence.why: This is the authoritative schema definition for evidence results that all agents must follow.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES is a Set with four allowed values: 'project', 'external', 'user', 'unknown'
evidence.why: This is the canonical list of valid scope values used throughout the system.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines three agent roles: explorer (find primary project facts), cross-checker (independently verify claims), verifier (check gathered evidence supports readiness). All have writes_code=false and result_schema='yolo.demand.evidence_result.v1'
evidence.why: This defines the agent protocols that enforce scope separation and prevent mutation.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope() function classifies evidence records by scope using explicit field check, URL pattern detection, source field inference, and path presence fallback. Returns normalized scope: project, external, user, or unknown.
evidence.why: This is the core scope classification logic that validates evidence records against the four allowed categories.

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope() checks if record has a valid scope from VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES. evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator() checks if record has a non-URL path field.
evidence.why: These helper functions validate that evidence records have proper scope declarations and project locators.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues() returns EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker for evidence records without declared valid scope. Blocker message: 'evidence records must declare scope as project, external, user, or unknown'
evidence.why: This generates explicit blockers when evidence records lack scope declarations, enforcing the scope requirement.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() returns PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED if evidence marked as project scope but lacks path locator. Returns PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE if evidence is only external/unknown when project facts are needed.
evidence.why: This enforces that project-scope evidence must have concrete file paths and blocks external-only evidence for existing-project claims.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness() integrates scope checking into readiness validation. Calls evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues() and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() for each complete evidence result. Adds blockers to readiness report.
evidence.why: This is the main readiness function that ensures scope validation is part of PRD readiness gates.

evidence.summary: VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES is duplicated in evidence-dispatch.ts as a Set with four values: 'project', 'external', 'user', 'unknown'
evidence.why: This shows scope validation is consistent across router and dispatch modules.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors() function returns error messages for evidence records that don't declare scope as one of the four valid values. Used in result normalization.
evidence.why: This validates evidence scope at dispatch time before accepting agent results.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt() generates agent prompts with explicit scope rules: 'Every evidence record must include scope: project, external, user, or unknown.' and 'Project facts require project-scoped evidence from code, tests, docs, config, logs, or artifacts, with a repo-relative path or file locator.'
evidence.why: This shows scope requirements are communicated to agents in their instructions.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult() applies evidenceScopeErrors() validation. If scope errors found, sets status=blocked, recommendation=block, and adds scope errors to missing array with error_code=EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED.
evidence.why: This shows that scope validation immediately blocks agent results with invalid scope declarations.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() and diffBoundarySnapshots() create filesystem digests before and after agent execution to detect unauthorized mutations. Used in boundary audit.
evidence.why: This is the boundary audit mechanism that prevents agents from modifying target project files during evidence gathering.

evidence.summary: Boundary audit calls buildBoundarySnapshot() before and after all agent executions, then diffBoundarySnapshots() to detect changes. Sets result.boundary.project_mutation='violated' if any changes detected outside allowed_write_roots.
evidence.why: This enforces the boundary contract that evidence agents cannot modify target project files.

evidence.summary: If boundary audit detects violations, generates BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers added to readiness, sets prd_ready=false, and status=blocked.
evidence.why: This shows boundary violations block PRD readiness, enforcing the read-only evidence gathering requirement.

evidence.summary: projectEvidence() helper function creates evidence records with scope='project', source='project_code', and required path field.
evidence.why: Test helper shows proper evidence record structure with scope declaration.

evidence.summary: Test 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' creates evidence with scope='external', source='web-search', url field. Expects readiness.prd_ready=false and blocker code=PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE.
evidence.why: Test verifies that external-only evidence is rejected for existing-project claims.

evidence.summary: Test 'project evidence requires a concrete project locator' creates evidence with scope='project', source='repo-docs' but no path field. Expects readiness.prd_ready=false and blocker code=PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED.
evidence.why: Test verifies that project-scope evidence without a path locator is rejected.

evidence.summary: Test 'external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence' creates evidence with scope='project', source='project_docs', url='https://example.com/project-docs'. Expects readiness.prd_ready=false and blocker code=PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED.
evidence.why: Test verifies that external URLs cannot be passed off as project evidence even if labeled as project scope.

evidence.summary: Test 'evidence records must declare valid scope explicitly' creates evidence with path and source but no scope field. Expects readiness.prd_ready=false and blocker code=EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED.
evidence.why: Test verifies that evidence records without explicit scope declarations are rejected.

evidence.summary: Test 'non-missing status notes in missing arrays do not block readiness' puts 'No missing data identified.' in missing array. Expects readiness.prd_ready=true because status notes are filtered out by isNonMissingStatusItem().
evidence.why: Test verifies that status notes (not actual missing items) are correctly ignored and don't block readiness.

risks: If evidence scope validation logic fails, agents could submit invalid scope declarations that bypass blocker generation.
If boundary audit misses filesystem changes, agents could modify target project files during evidence gathering.

result.notes: Evidence schema, protocols, readiness contract, and tests all comprehensively distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes with explicit validation. Scope enforcement is implemented at multiple layers: agent instructions, result normalization, readiness validation, and boundary auditing.
